title: Fix cleanup for "Pause a bunch of running containers"
body:
When running integration tests in our CI, we observe a problem where paused containers
are not able to be stopped; and therefore cannot be cleaned up.  This leaves dangling mounts
and sometimes zombied conmon processes.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
LGTM

We should probably discuss tomorrow whether `rm -f` should unpause if a container is paused. It seems counter intuitive that a container can stick around after an `rm -af`

I am not sure they should unpause them, but they do need to kill them.  Is there any way to kill these processes other then unpause?  The risk in unpause would be that the container process actually does something before being stopped/killed.
Should we just merge this one to allow the tests to pass?
LGTM, let's merge this as soon as possible and possibly unblock other PRs.  Thanks @baude!
LGTM and happy green test buttons.

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Nice find @baude! It's possible PAPR could've helped here by providing a list of processes still running on timeouts? Anyway, happy to see it sorted out.
